hallucinations_detected.text: Serializability: Ensured, because every transaction holds an exclusive lock on the data it writes until the end of the transaction, preventing any other transaction from accessing the same data until the lock is released.
Conflict-Serializability: Ensured, as a consequence of serializability.
Recoverability: Ensured, because transactions hold locks until the end, preventing other transactions from accessing data that might be rolled back.
Avoidance of Cascading Aborts: Ensured, because transactions do not release locks until the end, so there's no dependency on other transactions' locks that could lead to cascading aborts.

Recoverability: Not ensured, because a transaction may read data from another transaction that has not yet committed, and if the writing transaction aborts, the reading transaction may have already committed, leading to an inconsistent state.
Avoidance of Cascading Aborts: Not ensured, because a transaction may have read data from another transaction that later aborts, potentially requiring the first transaction to also abort.
